title: L-O-V-E
why_this_song: L-O-V-E by Nat King Cole (1964) — selected for the English-through-music catalog. Use the sing-along prompts and exercises to drill pronunciation, vocabulary, and listening comprehension.

vocab_hooks.note: Appears 2 times; first heard in: "V is very, very extraordinary"
Appears 2 times; first heard in: "E is even more than anyone that you adore and"
Appears 2 times; first heard in: "E is even more than anyone that you adore and"
Appears 2 times; first heard in: "Love is all that I can give to you"
Appears 2 times; first heard in: "Love is more than just a game for two"

teaching_notes_md:
## Teaching notes

- Times are estimated from community-synced lyrics and may be off by 1–2 seconds.
- Start with the sing-along prompt, then move to listen-and-fill.
- For B1+ students, focus on the discussion questions.
copyright_notice: Short fair-use lyric excerpts only (Brazilian Lei 9.610/98 art. 46 VIII). No full lyrics stored.

excerpts.text: O is for the only one I see
Love was made for me and you
Take my heart and please don't break it

exercises.prompt_en: If you could change the ending of the story told in "L-O-V-E", what would happen instead?
exercises.prompt_pt: Se você pudesse mudar o final da história contada em "L-O-V-E", o que aconteceria?

exercises.prompt_en: Describe the mood of "L-O-V-E" in three adjectives. What part of the song makes you feel that way?
exercises.prompt_pt: Descreva o clima de "L-O-V-E" em três adjetivos. Que parte da música te faz sentir isso?

exercises.prompt_en: Who in your life would you dedicate "L-O-V-E" to, and what line captures what you want to say to them?
exercises.prompt_pt: Para quem da sua vida você dedicaria "L-O-V-E", e qual verso resume o que você quer dizer para essa pessoa?
